revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tenson Kōrin]][[en:Tenson kōrin]][[fr:Tenson kōrin]][[id:Tenson kōrin]][[ja:天孫降臨]][[ko:천손강림]][[ru:Тенсон корин]][[th:เท็นซนโคริน]][[uk:Тенсон корін]][[zh:天孫降臨]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1084733]]
Wikidata name: tenson kōrin
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Short description|Japanese mythological event}}
{{More citations needed|date=August 2021}}
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{Nihongo|'''''tenson kōrin'''''|天孫降臨}} is the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]; according to legend, the direct place of descent is at [[:en:Takachiho-gawara|Takachiho-gawara]] in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kyushu_okinawa/kirishima/kanko/1348/|title=Takachiho-gawara {{!}} Kirishima {{!}} Japan Travel Guide - Japan Hoppers|last=Guide|first=Japan Hoppers Travel|website=Japan Hoppers - Free Japan Travel Guide|language=en|access-date=2019-02-05}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Nanzan Guide to Japanese Religions|year=2006|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3002-1|pages=131|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=4vuHBV0FpIIC&pg=PA131|editor1=Chilson, Clark |editor2=Swanson, Paul }}</ref> Following the ''tenson kōrin'', Ninigi's son, [[Hoori]], was born.

==Three generations of Hyuga==
After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>

{{Three generations of Hyuga}}

== Alternate tellings ==
In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythological event}}
{{More citations needed|date=August 2021}}
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{Nihongo|'''''tenson kōrin'''''|天孫降臨}} is the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]; according to legend, the direct place of descent is at [[:en:Takachiho-gawara|Takachiho-gawara]] in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kyushu_okinawa/kirishima/kanko/1348/|title=Takachiho-gawara {{!}} Kirishima {{!}} Japan Travel Guide - Japan Hoppers|last=Guide|first=Japan Hoppers Travel|website=Japan Hoppers - Free Japan Travel Guide|language=en|access-date=2019-02-05}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Nanzan Guide to Japanese Religions|year=2006|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3002-1|pages=131|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=4vuHBV0FpIIC&pg=PA131|editor1=Chilson, Clark |editor2=Swanson, Paul }}</ref> Following the ''tenson kōrin'', Ninigi's son, [[Hoori]], was born.

==Three generations of Hyuga==
After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>

{{Three generations of Hyuga}}

== Alternate tellings ==
In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythological event}}
{{More citations needed|date=August 2021}}
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{Nihongo|'''''tenson kōrin'''''|天孫降臨}} is the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]; according to legend, the direct place of descent is at [[:en:Takachiho-gawara|Takachiho-gawara]] in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kyushu_okinawa/kirishima/kanko/1348/|title=Takachiho-gawara {{!}} Kirishima {{!}} Japan Travel Guide - Japan Hoppers|last=Guide|first=Japan Hoppers Travel|website=Japan Hoppers - Free Japan Travel Guide|language=en|access-date=2019-02-05}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Nanzan Guide to Japanese Religions|year=2006|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3002-1|pages=131|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=4vuHBV0FpIIC&pg=PA131|editor1=Chilson, Clark |editor2=Swanson, Paul }}</ref> Following the ''tenson kōrin'', Ninigi's son, [[Hoori]], was born.

==Three generations of Hyuga==
After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>

{{Three generations of Hyuga}}

== Alternate tellings ==
In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tenson Kōrin]][[en:Tenson kōrin]][[fr:Tenson kōrin]][[id:Tenson kōrin]][[ja:天孫降臨]][[ko:천손강림]][[ru:Тенсон корин]][[th:เท็นซนโคริน]][[uk:Тенсон корін]][[zh:天孫降臨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1084733]] Wikidata name: tenson kōrin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese mythological event}} {{More citations needed|date=August 2021}} In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{Nihongo|'''''tenson kōrin'''''|天孫降臨}} is the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]; according to legend, the direct place of descent is at [[:en:Takachiho-gawara|Takachiho-gawara]] in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kyushu_okinawa/kirishima/kanko/1348/|title=Takachiho-gawara {{!}} Kirishima {{!}} Japan Travel Guide - Japan Hoppers|last=Guide|first=Japan Hoppers Travel|website=Japan Hoppers - Free Japan Travel Guide|language=en|access-date=2019-02-05}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Nanzan Guide to Japanese Religions|year=2006|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3002-1|pages=131|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=4vuHBV0FpIIC&pg=PA131|editor1=Chilson, Clark |editor2=Swanson, Paul }}</ref> Following the ''tenson kōrin'', Ninigi's son, [[Hoori]], was born.
==Three generations of Hyuga== After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Alternate tellings == In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

==Three generations of Hyuga== After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>

== Alternate tellings == In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tenson Kōrin]][[en:Tenson kōrin]][[fr:Tenson kōrin]][[id:Tenson kōrin]][[ja:天孫降臨]][[ko:천손강림]][[ru:Тенсон корин]][[th:เท็นซนโคริน]][[uk:Тенсон корін]][[zh:天孫降臨]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1084733]]
Wikidata name: tenson kōrin
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Short description|Japanese mythological event}}
{{More citations needed|date=August 2021}}
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{Nihongo|'''''tenson kōrin'''''|天孫降臨}} is the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]; according to legend, the direct place of descent is at [[:en:Takachiho-gawara|Takachiho-gawara]] in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kyushu_okinawa/kirishima/kanko/1348/|title=Takachiho-gawara {{!}} Kirishima {{!}} Japan Travel Guide - Japan Hoppers|last=Guide|first=Japan Hoppers Travel|website=Japan Hoppers - Free Japan Travel Guide|language=en|access-date=2019-02-05}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Nanzan Guide to Japanese Religions|year=2006|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3002-1|pages=131|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=4vuHBV0FpIIC&pg=PA131|editor1=Chilson, Clark |editor2=Swanson, Paul }}</ref> Following the ''tenson kōrin'', Ninigi's son, [[Hoori]], was born.

==Three generations of Hyuga==
After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>

{{Three generations of Hyuga}}

== Alternate tellings ==
In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

==Three generations of Hyuga==
After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>

{{Three generations of Hyuga}}

== Alternate tellings ==
In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tenson Kōrin]][[en:Tenson kōrin]][[fr:Tenson kōrin]][[id:Tenson kōrin]][[ja:天孫降臨]][[ko:천손강림]][[ru:Тенсон корин]][[th:เท็นซนโคริน]][[uk:Тенсон корін]][[zh:天孫降臨]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1084733]]
Wikidata name: tenson kōrin
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Short description|Japanese mythological event}}
{{More citations needed|date=August 2021}}
In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{Nihongo|'''''tenson kōrin'''''|天孫降臨}} is the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]; according to legend, the direct place of descent is at [[:en:Takachiho-gawara|Takachiho-gawara]] in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kyushu_okinawa/kirishima/kanko/1348/|title=Takachiho-gawara {{!}} Kirishima {{!}} Japan Travel Guide - Japan Hoppers|last=Guide|first=Japan Hoppers Travel|website=Japan Hoppers - Free Japan Travel Guide|language=en|access-date=2019-02-05}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Nanzan Guide to Japanese Religions|year=2006|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3002-1|pages=131|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=4vuHBV0FpIIC&pg=PA131|editor1=Chilson, Clark |editor2=Swanson, Paul }}</ref> Following the ''tenson kōrin'', Ninigi's son, [[Hoori]], was born.

==Three generations of Hyuga==
After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>

{{Three generations of Hyuga}}

== Alternate tellings ==
In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tenson Kōrin]][[en:Tenson kōrin]][[fr:Tenson kōrin]][[id:Tenson kōrin]][[ja:天孫降臨]][[ko:천손강림]][[ru:Тенсон корин]][[th:เท็นซนโคริน]][[uk:Тенсон корін]][[zh:天孫降臨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tenson Kōrin]][[en:Tenson kōrin]][[fr:Tenson kōrin]][[id:Tenson kōrin]][[ja:天孫降臨]][[ko:천손강림]][[ru:Тенсон корин]][[th:เท็นซนโคริน]][[uk:Тенсон корін]][[zh:天孫降臨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tenson Kōrin]][[en:Tenson kōrin]][[fr:Tenson kōrin]][[id:Tenson kōrin]][[ja:天孫降臨]][[ko:천손강림]][[ru:Тенсон корин]][[th:เท็นซนโคริน]][[uk:Тенсон корін]][[zh:天孫降臨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1084733]] Wikidata name: tenson kōrin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese mythological event}} {{More citations needed|date=August 2021}} In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{Nihongo|'''''tenson kōrin'''''|天孫降臨}} is the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]; according to legend, the direct place of descent is at [[:en:Takachiho-gawara|Takachiho-gawara]] in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kyushu_okinawa/kirishima/kanko/1348/|title=Takachiho-gawara {{!}} Kirishima {{!}} Japan Travel Guide - Japan Hoppers|last=Guide|first=Japan Hoppers Travel|website=Japan Hoppers - Free Japan Travel Guide|language=en|access-date=2019-02-05}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Nanzan Guide to Japanese Religions|year=2006|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3002-1|pages=131|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=4vuHBV0FpIIC&pg=PA131|editor1=Chilson, Clark |editor2=Swanson, Paul }}</ref> Following the ''tenson kōrin'', Ninigi's son, [[Hoori]], was born.
==Three generations of Hyuga== After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Alternate tellings == In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

==Three generations of Hyuga== After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Alternate tellings == In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

==Three generations of Hyuga== After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>

== Alternate tellings == In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tenson Kōrin]][[en:Tenson kōrin]][[fr:Tenson kōrin]][[id:Tenson kōrin]][[ja:天孫降臨]][[ko:천손강림]][[ru:Тенсон корин]][[th:เท็นซนโคริน]][[uk:Тенсон корін]][[zh:天孫降臨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tenson Kōrin]][[en:Tenson kōrin]][[fr:Tenson kōrin]][[id:Tenson kōrin]][[ja:天孫降臨]][[ko:천손강림]][[ru:Тенсон корин]][[th:เท็นซนโคริน]][[uk:Тенсон корін]][[zh:天孫降臨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tenson Kōrin]][[en:Tenson kōrin]][[fr:Tenson kōrin]][[id:Tenson kōrin]][[ja:天孫降臨]][[ko:천손강림]][[ru:Тенсон корин]][[th:เท็นซนโคริน]][[uk:Тенсон корін]][[zh:天孫降臨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1084733]] Wikidata name: tenson kōrin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese mythological event}} {{More citations needed|date=August 2021}} In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{Nihongo|'''''tenson kōrin'''''|天孫降臨}} is the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]; according to legend, the direct place of descent is at {{ill|Takachiho-gawara|en|Takachiho-gawara|WD=Q10566710|lt=Takachiho-gawara}} in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kyushu_okinawa/kirishima/kanko/1348/|title=Takachiho-gawara {{!}} Kirishima {{!}} Japan Travel Guide - Japan Hoppers|last=Guide|first=Japan Hoppers Travel|website=Japan Hoppers - Free Japan Travel Guide|language=en|access-date=2019-02-05}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Nanzan Guide to Japanese Religions|year=2006|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3002-1|pages=131|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=4vuHBV0FpIIC&pg=PA131|editor1=Chilson, Clark |editor2=Swanson, Paul }}</ref> Following the ''tenson kōrin'', Ninigi's son, [[Hoori]], was born.
==Three generations of Hyuga== After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Alternate tellings == In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

==Three generations of Hyuga== After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Alternate tellings == In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythological event}} {{More citations needed|date=August 2021}} In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{Nihongo|'''''tenson kōrin'''''|天孫降臨}} is the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]; according to legend, the direct place of descent is at {{ill|Takachiho-gawara|en|Takachiho-gawara|WD=Q10566710|lt=Takachiho-gawara}} in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kyushu_okinawa/kirishima/kanko/1348/|title=Takachiho-gawara {{!}} Kirishima {{!}} Japan Travel Guide - Japan Hoppers|last=Guide|first=Japan Hoppers Travel|website=Japan Hoppers - Free Japan Travel Guide|language=en|access-date=2019-02-05}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Nanzan Guide to Japanese Religions|year=2006|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3002-1|pages=131|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=4vuHBV0FpIIC&pg=PA131|editor1=Chilson, Clark |editor2=Swanson, Paul }}</ref> Following the ''tenson kōrin'', Ninigi's son, [[Hoori]], was born.
==Three generations of Hyuga== After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Alternate tellings == In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythological event}} {{More citations needed|date=August 2021}} In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{Nihongo|'''''tenson kōrin'''''|天孫降臨}} is the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]; according to legend, the direct place of descent is at {{ill|Takachiho-gawara|en|Takachiho-gawara|WD=Q10566710|lt=Takachiho-gawara|1=Takachiho-gawara}} in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kyushu_okinawa/kirishima/kanko/1348/|title=Takachiho-gawara {{!}} Kirishima {{!}} Japan Travel Guide - Japan Hoppers|last=Guide|first=Japan Hoppers Travel|website=Japan Hoppers - Free Japan Travel Guide|language=en|access-date=2019-02-05}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Nanzan Guide to Japanese Religions|year=2006|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3002-1|pages=131|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=4vuHBV0FpIIC&pg=PA131|editor1=Chilson, Clark |editor2=Swanson, Paul }}</ref> Following the ''tenson kōrin'', Ninigi's son, [[Hoori]], was born.
==Three generations of Hyuga== After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Alternate tellings == In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Alternate tellings == In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythological event}} {{More citations needed|date=August 2021}} In [[Japanese mythology]], the {{Nihongo|'''''tenson kōrin'''''|天孫降臨}} is the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]; according to legend, the direct place of descent is at {{ill|Takachiho-gawara|en|Takachiho-gawara|WD=Q10566710|lt=Takachiho-gawara|1=Takachiho-gawara}} in Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kyushu_okinawa/kirishima/kanko/1348/|title=Takachiho-gawara {{!}} Kirishima {{!}} Japan Travel Guide - Japan Hoppers|last=Guide|first=Japan Hoppers Travel|website=Japan Hoppers - Free Japan Travel Guide|language=en|access-date=2019-02-05}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Nanzan Guide to Japanese Religions|year=2006|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-3002-1|pages=131|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=4vuHBV0FpIIC&pg=PA131|editor1=Chilson, Clark |editor2=Swanson, Paul }}</ref> Following the ''tenson kōrin'', Ninigi's son, [[Hoori]], was born.
==Three generations of Hyuga== After the Tenson Korin there were the Three Generations of Hyuga until [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] when the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} was founded.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Alternate tellings == In some versions of this story, more gods came down from heaven besides Ninigi-no-Mikoto.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sonoda |first=Minoru |title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami |last2=Mogi |first2=Sakae |publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]] |year=1997 |isbn=9784056016291 |pages=68, 69 |language=ja |oclc=42978057 |author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
